title: Clean Fuels Weekly Commentary: LCFS Trading Volumes Surge WoW; Credit and RIN Prices Show Mixed Trends
content:
This Weekâ€™s Highlights

(29th December 2025 - 4th January 2026)
  • A total of 1,392,430 credits were exchanged as Type 1 trades, a rise of 394.77% week-over-week (WoW), and all non-zero trades witnessed an increase of 174.19% WoW as 2,152,232 credits were traded in CA LCFS.
  • The weekly average CA LCFS credit price stood at $54.34/MT for Type 1 transfers, recording a 0.69% rise WoW; prices of all non-zero transfers saw a fall of 0.26%, priced at $54.23/MT.
  • The average LCFS spot credit price rose by 2.05% WoW; on 6th January, the spot price stood at $60.00/MT, and the benchmark price stood at $64.50/MT.
  • D3 RIN prices fell by 0.42% WoW. Both D4 and D6 RIN prices rose by 1.98% WoW, and D5 RIN prices rose by 2.00% WoW.

cCarbonâ€™s Market View

Last week, the California Air Resources Board (CARB) released the sunset of the biodiesel in-use requirements which provides user-friendly public guidance on how the California Air Resources Board (CARB) will estimate the penetration of new technology diesel engines (NTDE) in the California heavy-duty on-road diesel vehicle fleet under CARBâ€™s Regulation on the Commercialization of Alternative Diesel Fuels (ADF regulation). Once the thresholds above have been reached, the CARB will issue Executive Orders certifying that biodiesel in-use requirements are no longer in effect for the on-road and off-road sectors.

CA LCFS Weekly Price and Volume Trend

[caption id="attachment_609717" align="alignnone" width="634"] Table 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average Type 1 transfer prices recorded a 0.69% WoW increase, rising from $53.97 to $54.34 this week, and all non-zero transfer prices fell from $54.37 to $54.23, recording a fall of 0.26%. Type 1 trades witnessed 1,392,430 credits exchanged, recording a 394.77% rise WoW. The total transaction volumes for all non-zero trades saw an increase of 174.19% WoW, reaching 2,152,232 credits. [caption id="attachment_609718" align="alignnone" width="1100"] Fig 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption]

CA LCFS Daily Average Spot Price

[caption id="attachment_609719" align="alignnone" width="1100"] Fig 2. California LCFS Weekly average spot prices and benchmark (*CC.Info OTC: Numbers are an average of broker prices reported to CC.info)[/caption] Last week, the average LCFS spot credit prices rose by 2.05% WoW, reaching $55.88/MT. On 6th January, the spot price stood at $60.00/MT. The benchmark price, representing the future price of contracts expiring at the end of 2026, stood at $64.50/MT on 6th January.

LCFS Weekly Trade Frequency and Volume

[caption id="attachment_609720" align="alignnone" width="1300"] Fig 3. Trade frequency and volume, Source: California ARB[/caption] As per the data reported by CARB last week, 75 trades were reported in the market, with 9 high-volume trades. The highest transfer by price was $80.00 for a Type 2 transaction at a volume of 5,000 credits. The lowest transfer by price was $46.00 per credit for a Type 1 transaction reported for 5,000 credits. The largest volume of transfers involved 430,000 credits for a Type 1 transaction, priced at $54.50. The smallest volume of transfers involved 1,752 credits for a Type 1 transaction priced at $55.00.

CA LCFS Monthly Volumes and Price Trend

The average spot Type 1 price stood at $50.91 in December so far, falling from $52.83 in November. As of 13th December 2025, trade volumes for the month reached 3.00M.

CA LCFS Weekly Volume Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_609722" align="alignnone" width="1300"] Fig 5. Volume Price Trend indicator for the CA LCFS market, Source: California ARB[/caption] The Volume Price Trend Indicator (VPT) helps identify the parity between supply and demand for a stock. The idea behind the indicator is to multiply the market volume of LCFS credits by the percentage change in its price. If the credit price declines, the value of the indicator falls. Across 29th December 2025 â€“ 4th January 2026, total VPT fell by 0.48% WoW, with a fall in all non-zero credit prices for the last week.

Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS): RIN Weekly Price Trend

RINs are credits used for compliance and are the â€œcurrencyâ€ of the RFS program. Renewable fuel producers generate RINs, market participants trade RINs, and obligated parties obtain and then ultimately retire RINs for the compliance list. D3 RIN prices fell by 0.42% WoW. Both D4 and D6 RIN prices rose by 1.98% WoW, and D5 RIN prices rose by 2.00% WoW. D3: Cellulosic biofuel D4: Biomass-based diesel D5: Advanced biofuels (produced from any type of renewable biomass except corn starch ethanol) D6: Conventional renewable biofuel ethanol derived from corn starch, or any other qualifying renewable fuel

Upcoming DatesÂ 

Key Clean Fuels Market Developments This Week

  • Discussion Paper: Driving Effective Carbon Markets in Canada: As announced in the Climate Competitiveness Strategy, Canada is engaging provinces and territories to improve Canada's industrial carbon pricing systems. As part of that initiative, this paper seeks feedback on the federal â€˜benchmark', which sets the minimum national stringency standards that all provincial and territorial systems must meet while allowing flexibility in design to address jurisdictional circumstances.
  • Sunset Estimation for Biodiesel In-Use Requirements: The sunset of the biodiesel in-use requirements occurred on December 31, 2025. The purpose of this document is to provide user-friendly public guidance on how the California Air Resources Board (CARB) will estimate the penetration of new technology diesel engines (NTDE) in the California heavy-duty on-road diesel vehicle fleet under CARBâ€™s Regulation on the Commercialization of Alternative Diesel Fuels (ADF regulation).CARB will issue Executive Orders certifying that biodiesel in-use requirements are no longer in effect for the on-road and off-road sectors once the thresholds above have been reached.
  • Clean Fuels Alliance America Applauds CARBâ€™s Decision to Sunset Outdated Biodiesel Restrictions: Clean Fuels Alliance America has applauded the California Air Resources Boardâ€™s (CARB) decision to sunset the oxides of nitrogen (NOx) mitigation requirement for biodiesel blends up to B20 under the Alternative Diesel Fuel (ADF) regulation, effective January 1, 2026. Previously, regulations required producers to mix at least 55% renewable diesel with biodiesel blends between B6 and B20 to address emissions in older heavy-duty vehicles. Clean Fuels had advocated this change since 2022, noting that CARBâ€™s own data confirmed these restrictions were no longer necessary. This decision removes a significant barrier that had kept overall biodiesel blends below 10%, offering a cost-effective compliance option for fleet operators. Jeffrey Earl, Director of State Governmental Affairs, called the move a constructive step that provides regulatory clarity, though the organization continues to challenge other recent CARB actions to ensure policies remain grounded in sound data and affordability.
  • Advanced Biofuels Canada Association Welcomes Federal Biofuels Production Incentive Coming into Force: "The Biofuels Production Incentive takes effect, providing immediate support to protect Canadian biofuel production capacity and strengthen Canadaâ€™s energy security,â€ said Fred Ghatala, President of Advanced Biofuels Canada Association.
